title: Lotus strigosus
intro: The Lotus strigosus, also known as the hairy lotus or lotus astringens, is a perennial plant belonging to the Nelumbo genus, native to eastern Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lotus strigosus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lotus strigosus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to clear heat and dry dampness in the body.
taxonomy: Lotus strigosus is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to parts of North America. It is a perennial plant that grows in wet environments.
